quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Electron transport chain"?

options.text: A shared derived character that defines a clade; evidence of common ancestry (e.g., feathers in birds)
A series of protein complexes (I, II, III, IV) embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane that transfer electrons and pump protons
A nucleotide change that creates a premature stop codon, producing a truncated, usually nonfunctional protein
Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes during prophase I of meiosis; increases genetic variation

quiz_questions.explanation: "Electron transport chain" is defined as: A series of protein complexes (I, II, III, IV) embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane that transfer electrons and pump protons. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Oxidative phosphorylation"?

options.text: ATP synthesis driven by the proton gradient across the inner mitochondrial membrane; produces ~30-32 ATP per glucose
Membrane-bound organelle containing chromatin (DNA + histones); site of DNA replication, transcription, and ribosome assembly (nucleolus)
Both alleles are fully expressed in the heterozygote (e.g., Iᴬ Iᴮ blood type produces both A and B antigens)
A gene encoding a protein that inhibits cell division (e.g., p53 triggers apoptosis in damaged cells, Rb blocks G1/S transition)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Oxidative phosphorylation" is defined as: ATP synthesis driven by the proton gradient across the inner mitochondrial membrane; produces ~30-32 ATP per glucose. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "ATP synthase"?

options.text: Random orientation of homologous pairs at metaphase I, producing 2ⁿ possible gamete combinations (n = haploid number)
A normal gene that promotes cell growth; mutations can convert it to an oncogene, causing uncontrolled cell division
A mathematical model estimating a population's probability of persistence over a specified time period, incorporating demographic and environmental variability
The enzyme that synthesizes ATP as H⁺ flows down its concentration gradient from the intermembrane space back to the matrix

quiz_questions.explanation: "ATP synthase" is defined as: The enzyme that synthesizes ATP as H⁺ flows down its concentration gradient from the intermembrane space back to the matrix. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Proton-motive force"?

options.text: The combination of the H⁺ concentration gradient and electrical potential across the inner mitochondrial membrane that drives ATP synthesis
Both alleles are fully expressed in the heterozygote (e.g., Iᴬ Iᴮ blood type produces both A and B antigens)
The rate at which producers store energy as biomass minus their own respiratory losses; NPP = GPP - respiration
Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase; the most abundant enzyme on Earth; fixes CO₂ but also has oxygenase activity (photorespiration)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Proton-motive force" is defined as: The combination of the H⁺ concentration gradient and electrical potential across the inner mitochondrial membrane that drives ATP synthesis. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Final electron acceptor"?

options.text: The variety of life at three levels: genetic diversity (within species), species diversity (number and relative abundance), and ecosystem diversity
The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes
O₂; accepts electrons at the end of the ETC and combines with H⁺ to form water; without O₂, the ETC stops
5' cap (7-methylguanosine) and 3' poly-A tail added for stability and export; introns spliced out by spliceosomes

quiz_questions.explanation: "Final electron acceptor" is defined as: O₂; accepts electrons at the end of the ETC and combines with H⁺ to form water; without O₂, the ETC stops. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A series of protein complexes (I, II, III, IV) embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane that transfer electrons and pump protons"?

options.text: Electron transport chain
Photorespiration
Density-dependent inhibition
Alternative splicing

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Electron transport chain".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "ATP synthesis driven by the proton gradient across the inner mitochondrial membrane; produces ~30-32 ATP per glucose"?

options.text: Gel electrophoresis
Oxidative phosphorylation
Keystone species
Central dogma

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Oxidative phosphorylation".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The enzyme that synthesizes ATP as H⁺ flows down its concentration gradient from the intermembrane space back to the matrix"?

options.text: Genotype frequency
ATP synthase
Hydrogen bond
Tertiary structure

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "ATP synthase".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The combination of the H⁺ concentration gradient and electrical potential across the inner mitochondrial membrane that drives ATP synthesis"?

options.text: Proton-motive force
Substrate-level phosphorylation
Hardy-Weinberg conditions
Species richness

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Proton-motive force".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "O₂; accepts electrons at the end of the ETC and combines with H⁺ to form water; without O₂, the ETC stops"?

options.text: Mutagen
Final electron acceptor
Recombinant DNA
Photosystem II (P680)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Final electron acceptor".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain is accurate?

options.text: Protein denaturation (loss of 3D shape from heat, pH, or chemical changes) destroys function, demonstrating that structure determines function—a central theme in AP Biology.
Cyclin-dependent kinases (CDKs) activated by cyclins drive progression past checkpoints.
Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain (ETC) and oxidative phosphorylation occur on the inner mitochondrial membrane.
Regulation of Enzyme Activity controls metabolic pathways through allosteric regulation, competitive and noncompetitive inhibition, and feedback inhibition.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: Competitive inhibitors resemble the substrate and compete for the active site, while noncompetitive inhibitors bind elsewhere and reduce catalytic activity.
NADH and FADH₂ donate electrons to protein complexes (I-IV), which pump H⁺ from the matrix to the intermembrane space, creating a proton gradient.
Biodiversity and Conservation addresses the variety of life at genetic, species, and ecosystem levels and the threats to it.
Triglycerides (fats and oils) store energy (9 kcal/g) through ester bonds between glycerol and three fatty acids.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: Oxygen is the final electron acceptor, forming water.
NADP⁺ is reduced to NADPH at PSI.
These properties arise from water's bent molecular geometry and electronegativity difference between O and H.
Evolution and Evidence establishes that populations change over time through multiple converging lines of evidence.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: Saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acids differ in the presence of C=C double bonds, affecting fluidity and melting point.
ATP synthase uses the proton-motive force (chemiosmosis) to produce ~30-32 ATP per glucose.
In eukaryotes, pre-mRNA undergoes processing: 5' cap addition, 3' poly-A tail, and splicing to remove introns and join exons.
Enzyme activity follows Michaelis-Menten kinetics characterized by Vmax (maximum velocity) and Km (substrate concentration at half Vmax).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: The ETC produces the vast majority of ATP in aerobic respiration.
Sexual selection and artificial selection are special cases of selection that shape traits beyond survival.
Biotechnology Applications encompasses the tools and techniques used to manipulate DNA.
Evolution and Evidence establishes that populations change over time through multiple converging lines of evidence.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: Darwin's finch beak evolution on Daphne Major Island: drought years selected for larger beaks (to crack harder seeds), directly observed by Peter and Rosemary Grant over 40 years
Captive breeding and reintroduction programs recovering the California condor from 22 individuals (1982) to over 500, including 300+ in the wild
Cyanide poisoning blocking Complex IV of the ETC, preventing electron transfer to O₂ and halting all aerobic ATP production
Mass extinctions (e.g., K-Pg, 66 MYA) eliminating 75% of species but opening ecological niches that enabled mammalian diversification and eventual human evolution

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing using guide RNA complementary base pairing to target specific DNA sequences for precise genetic modification
Artificial selection in agriculture: selective breeding transforming teosinte (small, hard kernels) into modern corn over thousands of years of human-directed selection
Brown fat thermogenesis: uncoupling protein (UCP1) dissipates the proton gradient as heat instead of ATP, warming newborns and hibernating animals
Active transport of ions (Na⁺/K⁺ ATPase) using ATP to pump 3 Na⁺ out and 2 K⁺ in per cycle, maintaining the resting membrane potential essential for nerve impulses

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Biofuel production engineering algae with enhanced Calvin cycle efficiency to convert CO₂ into lipids for biodiesel
Insulin signaling pathway: insulin binding to its receptor triggers a kinase cascade leading to glucose transporter (GLUT4) insertion into cell membranes
Cancer epigenetics: abnormal DNA methylation patterns silencing tumor suppressor genes or activating oncogenes without DNA mutations
Mitochondrial diseases caused by mutations in ETC complex genes, affecting energy-demanding tissues (brain, heart, muscles) and causing conditions like Leigh syndrome

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain?

options.text: Cyanide poisoning blocking Complex IV of the ETC, preventing electron transfer to O₂ and halting all aerobic ATP production
Whale evolution documented through a remarkable fossil series: Pakicetus (land mammal) → Ambulocetus (walking whale) → Dorudon (aquatic) → modern whales over ~50 million years
Wildlife management using logistic growth models to calculate maximum sustainable yield for harvested populations (fisheries, deer management)
Stem cell differentiation controlled by sequential gene regulation, with the same genome producing >200 different specialized cell types in the human body

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain in practice?

options.text: HPV (human papillomavirus) causing cervical cancer by producing proteins that inactivate p53 and Rb tumor suppressors, disabling cell cycle checkpoints
Citrate as a food additive and preservative, derived from the first intermediate of the Krebs cycle, produced industrially by Aspergillus niger fermentation
Evaporative cooling in humans through sweating, utilizing water's high heat of vaporization (2,260 J/g) to dissipate excess body heat
Brown fat thermogenesis: uncoupling protein (UCP1) dissipates the proton gradient as heat instead of ATP, warming newborns and hibernating animals

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration: Electron Transport Chain.
